title: Decision Event
description: The minimal unit of governance evidence that enables post-incident reconstruction of automated decisions. Each property group resists a specific governance artifact degradation type. Version 0.3.0 requires explicit schema_version declaration on every event and preserves the v0.2 feature set: two-path human judgment design (primary human decisions vs algorithmic overrides), logic type classification, mandatory override tracking, tiered evidence, cryptographic integrity fields with generation sequence specification, and boundary contract extensions (data_contract, failure_mode).

schema_version.description: Semantic version of the Decision Event Schema this event conforms to.

decision_context.description: Property 1: Semantic completeness. Records inputs, alternatives, and constraints available at decision time. Resists Content Staleness degradation.

decision_id.description: Unique identifier for this decision event. May be a UUID or a human-readable slug.

decision_type.description: Domain-level category of decision (e.g., fraud_detection, policy_enforcement, autonomous_operation). Implementations define domain-appropriate values.

trigger.description: What initiated this decision (event, schedule, human request, upstream decision).

inputs.description: Structured input records available at decision time.

environment.description: Runtime context: system_id, system_version, configuration_hash, deployment_id.

available_inputs.description: Data sources and signals available to the decision-maker at the time of decision.

alternatives_considered.description: Decision alternatives that were evaluated before the final choice.

constraints_operative.description: Regulatory, technical, or organizational constraints that bounded the decision space.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property.

decision_logic.description: Property 2: Causal reconstructability. Records the logic applied to reach the decision. Resists Schema Drift degradation.

logic_type.description: How the decision was made. Core values form a closed set; domain-specific extensions use namespace-prefixed values (e.g., fintech:credit_scoring).

output.description: Final decision output. When an override occurs, this reflects the overridden outcome (matches human_override_record.overridden_output).

rule_path.description: For rule_based: ordered list of rules evaluated.

model_inference.description: For ml_inference: model identification and inference metadata.

policy_evaluation.description: For policy_evaluation: policy engine evaluation trace.

combination_method.description: For hybrid: how multiple logic sources were combined (voting, cascading, overriding, weighted).

rule_version.description: Version identifier of the rule set or model used.

parameters.description: Key parameters that governed the decision.

thresholds.description: Threshold values that triggered or influenced the decision.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property.

decision_boundary.description: Property 3: Cross-system coupling. Records contributing subsystems and dependencies. Resists Coverage Erosion degradation.

upstream_decisions.description: Decisions that fed into this one, each with its own boundary contract.

data_contract.description: Schema or format contract for data exchanged across this boundary.

failure_mode.description: Expected behavior when this boundary fails (e.g., fail-open, fail-closed, degrade).

downstream_consumers.description: Systems expected to consume this decision, each with its own boundary contract.

data_contract.description: Schema or format contract for data exchanged across this boundary.

failure_mode.description: Expected behavior when this boundary fails (e.g., fail-open, fail-closed, degrade).

contributing_subsystems.description: Internal subsystems that contributed to or were affected by the decision.

external_dependencies.description: External systems, data feeds, or services the decision depended on.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property.

decision_quality_indicators.description: Property 4: Evidence of decision quality. Records confidence, uncertainty, and risk assessment. Resists Metric Erosion degradation.

confidence_score.description: Aggregate confidence in the decision (0.0 to 1.0). Null when no score was computed.

data_quality.description: Quality of input data: completeness, freshness, known issues.

uncertainty_flags.description: Conditions flagged as uncertain at the time of decision.

ground_truth_available.description: Whether ground truth data was available at the time of decision.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property.

human_override_record.description: Property 5: Independence of human judgment. Records override decisions and rationale. Resists Override Accumulation degradation. override_occurred is mandatory at all evidence tiers for all logic types.

override_occurred.description: Whether a human override was applied to this decision. Mandatory at all evidence tiers for all logic types to prevent silent falsification of the governance record.

override_type.description: Type of override: approval, rejection, modification, escalation, deferral.

original_output.description: What the system would have decided without override.

override_rationale.description: Free-text or structured rationale for the override.

time_to_override.description: Duration between system output and human action.

override_decision.description: What the human override changed relative to the automated recommendation. Null when override was structurally impossible or absent by design.

independence_assessment.description: Whether the override was made independently or under organizational pressure.

basis_for_deviation.description: The stated rationale for deviating from the automated recommendation.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property.

temporal_metadata.description: Property 6: Temporal evidence. Records timestamps, cryptographic integrity, and evidence tier. Resists Ground Truth Delay degradation.

event_timestamp.description: When the decision was made (ISO 8601, UTC).

processing_duration_ms.description: Time taken to reach the decision in milliseconds.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence within the generating system.

hash_chain.description: Cryptographic integrity chain for tamper evidence (forward integrity).

previous_hash.description: Hash of the previous DecisionEvent. Null for the first event in a chain.

current_hash.description: Hash of this DecisionEvent, computed over the canonicalized payload (RFC 8785) with current_hash excluded from input.

evidence_tier.description: Evidence completeness tier. lightweight: required fields only. sampled: context plus abbreviated logic. full: all property groups complete.

minimum_retention.description: ISO 8601 duration for minimum retention period (e.g., P6M for EU AI Act Art. 19).

digital_signature.description: Cryptographic signature from the generating system for non-repudiation of origin (NIST SP 800-53 AU-10). signature_value is computed over the canonicalized payload (RFC 8785) excluding both signature_value and current_hash; current_hash is then computed over the payload including signature_value. Non-repudiation may alternatively be provided by transport-layer mechanisms such as TEE attestation or mTLS.

signature_value.description: Digital signature over the canonicalized event (excludes both signature_value and current_hash from input).

evidence_availability_timestamp.description: When evidence about this decision became available for review.

ground_truth_arrival_timestamp.description: When ground truth about the decision outcome became known.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property.

allOf.title: Override output fields required when override occurred
Primary human decisions require attribution regardless of override
